The '''Lan system''' was a [[Star system/Legends|star system]] located within the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim's]] [[Hook Nebula/Legends|Hook Nebula]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]], centered around the [[Star/Legends|star]] [[Lan (star)|Lan]].

==Description==
The Lan system was located approximately three days and five hours' travel [[Time/Legends|time]] beyond the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] terminus of the [[Enarc Run/Legends|Enarc Run]]. The system contained five [[Planet/Legends|planets]], with the inner worlds—[[Lesser Galam]], [[Lan Fellov]], and [[Lan Tundi]]—being extraordinarily rich in ores and [[Mineral/Legends|minerals]]. The only planet to develop life was the fourth planet, [[Lan Barell/Legends|Lan Barell]]. The fifth planet, a gas giant, was named [[Greater Galam]].<ref name="LB" />

==History==
Sentient life developed on the fourth planet of the system in the form of the [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoid]] [[Qieg/Legends|Qieg]]. The system was left alone for a greater part of [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] history until around [[1300 BBY/Legends|1300 BBY]], when [[Scout/Legends|scouts]] from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] discovered the ore-rich nature of the system. Soon afterwards [[Gaminne Group, Inc.]] moved in and bought mining rights for the inner planets from the Qieg. [[Human/Legends|Human]] settlers were allowed to settle on Lan Barell under a charter that allowed them to build [[Shulell City]] on the fourth planet, [[Lan Barell/Legends|Lan Barell]].<ref name="LB" />

As centuries passed, the contents of this contract faded from the memories and the Human and Qieg cultures on Lan Barell began to merge to the point that, by the time of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the two races considered that the planet was jointly owned. When Gamine Group, Inc. ceased to exist, the mining operation were taken over by local conglomerates, as they were the only income for the otherwise poor system. The [[Human-Qieg Guild]], a consortium of corporations and companies, evolved as a new government. The ore was transported corewards, often to poor [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] systems, mostly directly from the planets where it was mined. Financially however, each deal ran through the [[Quilan Hive]] in Shulell City, which had become the center of commerce for the whole system. Attempts to diversify the economy by the Guild over the centuries as well the attempt to attract new settlers ended in failure. Over the time, the human population began to dwindle.<ref name="LB" />

When the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] emerged, Lan Barell became a save haven for refugees and exiles. This was because the system was relatively remote from any major [[Hyperspace route/Legends|trade routes]] and the government was rather lax concerning immigration and law enforcement. Around [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]] the Empire claimed the system, but it only showed up, evaluated the tax profits of the mining operations and then left again, leaving the locals alone. Aside from a small embassy in Shulell City, a representative of the local [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] visited the system twice a year.<ref name="LB" />
